assertions.text: No braking occurred immediately before the collision (vehicle was stationary and not moving).
Vehicle speed was 0 mph/kmh at the time of collision (we were stationary).
There was a single impact (one distinct heavy impact) to the car.
The impact was heavy (a single heavy impact struck the car).
The vehicle was stationary and waiting before the impact occurred (stationary before impact).
The car was struck by something (the car was impacted rather than striking another object).

verdicts.evidence: The vehicle showed a significant crash pulse on accel_x peaking at [ -5.862 g at t=11.01 s ](T2) and the accel_x integrated delta-V over the window is [ -54.4 km/h ](T3), which indicates a large deceleration rather than no braking; GPS speed also shows drops around the event from [45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0 s](T2), so the claim of no braking/stationary is contradicted.

GPS-derived speed statistics show a non-zero speed during the window with max speed [47.0 km/h](T3) and detected speed drops that include a change from [45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0 s](T2), so the vehicle was not at [0 km/h](T3) at impact.

Event detection found one primary impact spike on accel_x peaking at [t=11.01 s with magnitude 5.86 g](T2) and a single coincident accel_y spike at [t=11.01 s with magnitude 2.67 g](T2), consistent with a single distinct heavy impact.

The impact meets the high threshold with accel_x peak absolute deviation [5.862 g at t=11.01 s](T3) and accel_z deviation of [3.816 g at t=11.03 s](T3), and the accel_x integrated delta-V is large at [ -54.4 km/h ](T3), supporting characterization as a heavy impact.

GPS speed samples show the vehicle had non-zero speed in the window (first sample [0.0 km/h at t=0.0 s] and max [47.0 km/h](T3)), and a detected speed drop around the event from [45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0 s](T2), contradicting that the vehicle was stationary immediately before impact.

Sensors show a strong impact pulse on device-frame axes (accel_x peak [ -5.862 g at t=11.01 s ](T3) and accel_y peak [ -2.665 g at t=11.01 s ](T3)), but device-to-vehicle frame alignment is undocumented so the telemetry alone cannot determine whether the car was struck by another object or struck something else; this is therefore unverifiable.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a large high-g impact at [t=11.01 s](T2) with accel_x peak [ -5.862 g ](T3) and an integrated device-axis delta-V of [ -54.4 km/h ](T3); GPS speed samples and detected speed drops ([45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h](T2)) indicate the vehicle was moving before the event, so claims of being stationary are contradicted. Impact count and severity are supported by the IMU evidence; direction-of-strike is unverifiable from device-frame data.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned
